title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Runtime Contract: Shutdown Close Register
summary: A public server uses package defaults, has zero timeouts, trusts unlimited headers, enables cleartext HTTP/2 accidentally, serves weak TLS, and terminates active requests on deploy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo shutdown close register increment in explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.
Configure TLS identity, versions, certificates, ALPN, rotation, proxy termination, redirects, and plaintext policy with end-to-end scheme evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Shutdown Close Register · Theory Shutdown Close Register
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4666 in a internal administrative API crossing two trust zones. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.
